name: Regina Carter
abstract: Regina Carter (born August 6, 1966) is an American jazz violinist. She is the cousin of jazz saxophonist James Carter.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: That started our relationship. Then she got a gig there and called me to work with her. We worked off and on together in different situations.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Somebody like Regina Carter, she seems to know exactly what to do for herself. I think a lot of it has to do with knowing what to do and how to market... She’s got everything going for her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Then she might have replaced Regina Carter, who had been the violinist in that group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: I invited Regina Carter who, of course, knows what we're doing.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: Say for instance Detroit, where people like ... Regina Carter
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006
